task_completion.description: Create the required layers in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD Layer List panel shows the custom layers: BACKPLATE, DINRAIL, WIREDUCT, COMPONENTS, LABELS, and DIMENSIONS.

task_completion.description: Draw the main backplate and wire ducts
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large outer rectangle representing the 600x800 backplate is visible, along with inner rectangles or lines representing the wire ducts inset from the perimeter.

task_completion.description: Draw the three horizontal DIN rails
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three horizontal rectangular strips (DIN rails) are drawn across the middle area of the panel layout.

task_completion.description: Draw the electrical components on the DIN rails
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rectangular boxes representing components are placed on the DIN rails: 2 boxes on the top rail, 3 boxes on the middle rail, and 1 long rectangular strip on the bottom rail.

task_completion.description: Add component labels and the drawing title
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels (QF1, PLC1, KM1, KM2, KM3, TB1) are visible near their respective components, and a title 'MOTOR CONTROL PANEL - INTERNAL LAYOUT' (or similar) is present on the drawing.

task_completion.description: Add overall and rail position dimensions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines with measurements are visible indicating the overall width, overall height, and at least one vertical position for a rail.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing as a DXF file in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog displays the filename 'control_panel_layout.dxf' and indicates it is saved in '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools, interacting with the LibreCAD canvas, and using the layer/command panels, rather than just executing a script in the terminal to generate the DXF.
The screenshots show a step-by-step progression of the drawing being built (e.g., empty canvas -> layers added -> shapes drawn -> text added) rather than a completed file suddenly being opened.
The application interface visible in the screenshots is LibreCAD, not a text editor or a different CAD program.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must be created in LibreCAD and saved as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/control_panel_layout.dxf. It must contain exactly 6 layers: BACKPLATE, DINRAIL, WIREDUCT, COMPONENTS, LABELS, DIMENSIONS. The layout must include a 600x800mm backplate, 4 wire ducts, 3 horizontal DIN rails, and specific components. Required text labels are: QF1, PLC1, KM1, KM2, KM3, TB1, and 'MOTOR CONTROL PANEL'. Dimensions must be present for width, height, and at least one vertical rail position.
